text:
Black Fern Jazmin Felix-Hotham celebrates after the match.Photo:Iain McGregor / www.photosport.nz

The Kiwis ran in nine tries in theirlopsided quarterfinal against Chinaat a hot and steamy Stade de France and will now tackle the USA in the semifinal at 1.30am on Wednesday.

The Americans ended the hopes of Great Britain with a 17-7 win.

In the second semi Australia will take on Canada, who silenced the home crowd by beating France.

Meanwhile, Erika Fairweather finished seventh in 200m freestyle semifinal where the Australians Mollie O'Callaghan won gold with Ariarne Titmus taking the silver.

Aheartbroken Sam Gazehas spoken of how he tried to overcome a "horrendous" start and had nothing left, after climbing to sixth in a fast Olympic mountain biking medal final. "I emptied myself out there," he said.

The New Zealand equestrian eventing team finished outside medal contention, placing eighth overall as the competition concluded with show jumping at the Chateau de Versailles.

The Black Ferns Sevens scorched across for nine tries in a lopsided quarterfinal at the Stade de France.

Sam Gaze failed to overcome a "horrendous" start and had nothing left, after climbing to sixth in a fast Olympic mountain biking medal final.

Kiwi athletes are gunning for glory in women's rugby sevens, triathlon and swimming. Here's who to watch.

Another strong day on the water could spell a podium place as both men's and women's teams advance to the finals.

New Zealand and their three biggest rivals are all guaranteed quarter-final berths at the Olympic women's sevens tournament in Paris.

title: Olympics live: Black Ferns Sevens score big win, Erika Fairweather finishes 7th | RNZ News
description: The Kiwis ran in nine tries in their lopsided quarterfinal, while New Zealand's athletes had less luck elsewhere.
